name: Atama Soup
preparation_steps:
Step 1: If using fresh Atama leaves, slice them very thinly (or buy pre-sliced). Wash and squeeze them 2-3 times to remove the sticky, bitter juice. If using dry leaves, soak in water to soften, then squeeze.
Step 2: Palm Fruit: Boil palm fruits for 15-30 minutes, pound to separate the pulp from the nut, mix with hot water, and strain to extract the thick, oily, orange juice.
 Step 3:  Proteins & Periwinkles: Wash and season meat (goat/beef), stockfish, and ponmo with salt, onions, and seasoning cubes. Cook until tender. Wash periwinkles thoroughly. 
Step 4:  Cooking the Soup
Boil the Base: Pour the palm fruit extract into a large pot and bring to a boil for about 10-15 minutes until it thickens and the oil settles on top.
Step 4: Add Ingredients: Add the cooked assorted meats, stockfish, and smoked fish to the palm extract.
 Step 5: Seasoning: Add the ground crayfish, pepper, and periwinkles. Stir gently and let it simmer for 5–10 minutes.
Step 6: Add Atama Leaves: Add the sliced atama leaves. Stir, cover, and let it cook for another 5 minutes on medium heat.
 Step 7: Final Touch: Taste and add salt or more seasoning if necessary. The soup should have a medium consistency (neither too watery nor too thick

name: chicken noodle soup
description: chicken noodle
preparation_steps:
Cook it in the instant pot

    Press the sauté mode on the instant pot.
    Add olive oil, then add the onion and minced garlic for about a minute when it gets hot.
    3 tablespoon olive oil, ½ onion, 2 cloves garlic
    Add celery and stir for a few more minutes until it softens.
    2 stalks celery
    Add the chicken, water, chicken stock, and spices.
    1.5 lbs chicken drumsticks, 2 cups chicken stock, 2 cups of water, ½ teaspoon smoked paprika, 1 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon thyme, 1 teaspoon bouillon powder, ½ teaspoon cayenne pepper
    Switch the instant pot to manual mode and cook on high pressure for 20 mins.
    Do a quick release by switching the valve to the venting position.
    Take out the chicken, shred it, and put them back.
    Add the coconut aminos, taste for salt, and adjust spices as needed.
    3 tablespoon coconut aminos
    Switch the instant pot to sauté mode and add the shirataki noodles.
    2 packs shirataki noodles
    Simmer for about 5 minutes and garnish with parsley.
    Your keto soup recipe is ready to eat!

Cook it on the stove

    Follow the same steps as above, except the chicken cooks for 45 minutes.
    Cook the soup on medium high heat.

Cook it on the the crockpot/slow cooker

    Follow the same steps as above, except you cook the chicken on low for about 6-8 hours.

Notes
This recipe serves 8 and contains 2g net carbs per serving.

    Cook the chicken with the skin. You can remove the skin when you take it out to shred.
    When the soup cooks, do a quick release and not a natural release. In other words, open the release valve so the pressure can be released quickly.
    You can substitute coconut aminos with soy sauce.
    You can add your favorite fresh vegetables or fresh herbs to this soup for even more flavor.
    If meal prepping, don't forget to freeze the soup BEFORE you add the noodles.

name: Abacha
description: Abacha
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Prepare the Abacha: Soak the dried cassava flakes in hot water for 5–10 minutes until they soften. Rinse thoroughly with cold water to remove sand, then drain and set aside.
Step 2: Make the Sauce (Ncha): In a pot, mix the palm oil with about 2-3 tablespoons of dissolved potash water. Stir continuously with a spoon until the mixture becomes creamy and turns a bright orange color.
Step 3: Season the Base: Add the ground pepper, onions, ground crayfish, crushed stock cubes, ugba, ground ehu, and ogiri (if using) to the palm oil paste. Mix thoroughly.
Step 4: Combine: Add the softened, drained abacha into the pot and stir until every strand is properly coated with the sauce.
Step 5: Add Protein: Stir in the cooked kpomo, smoked fish, and other proteins.
Step 6: Adjust and Serve: Taste for salt and seasoning. Garnish with chopped onions, garden eggs, and utazi leaves.
Step 7: Optional Heating: While it can be eaten cold, you can lightly warm the Abacha on low heat for 2-3 minutes.

name: Egusi Soup
description: Egusi soup has a wonderfully complex flavor and is made with traditional West African ingredients and spices. It is a very thick soup and is more of a stew, as you can eat it with rice or other dishes. It is sometimes referred to as Egusi Stew.
preparation_steps:
If using smoked catfish, soak in hot water for 10 minutes.
    Break the dried catfish into smaller pieces and remove as many pieces of bones as you can.
    Chop the onions and spinach.
    Blend the tomatoes, red bell pepper, and habanero pepper. Add as little water as possible.

Make the Egusi Soup

    Heat the palm oil on medium heat for a few minutes, then add chopped onions. After it becomes translucent, add your blended tomato and pepper mix and stir.
    Add your smoked fish and cook on medium heat for about 10 minutes. Add broth or water and bring to a boil.
    Pour the ground egusi seeds into a bowl, add a sprinkling of water, and form them into balls. Then, add the egusi seeds to the soup and stir. Bring to a boil and let it cook for five more minutes.
    Add the goat meat, cow feet, crayfish, bouillon, and cayenne pepper. Taste for salt and add some if you need to.
    Simmer for 10 minutes on medium-high heat. Add the spinach and leave on low heat for 5 minutes.
    Serve with your choice of fufu and enjoy!

name: Mushroom Soup
description: Mushroom
preparation_steps:
hop the onions and mince the garlic.
    ½ cup onion, 1 clove garlic
    Slice the mushrooms, if they are whole.
    4 cups mushrooms

Make it on the stove

    Sauté onions and garlic in olive oil till translucent.
    ⅓ cup olive oil
    Add mushrooms, salt, and black pepper and stir for 5 minutes.
    ½ teaspoon salt, ½ teaspoon black pepper
    Add chicken stock, bouillon powder, cayenne pepper, and thyme.
    4 cups chicken broth, ½ teaspoon cayenne pepper, ½ teaspoon bouillon powder, ½ teaspoon thyme
    Stir and scrape the browned bits.
    Bring to boil and simmer for 10 minutes.
    Mix in heavy cream and cream cheese.
    ½ cup heavy whipping cream, 4 oz cream cheese
    Cook for 5 more minutes.
    Blend with an immersion blender or pour into a regular blender.
    Put back on the stove and adjust seasoning.
    Simmer for 3- 4 minutes on low heat.

Make it in the instant pot

    Sauté the onions and garlic in the instant pot on sauté mode.
    Add the mushrooms, salt, and black pepper and cook for about 5 minutes, stirring often.
    Add your chicken stock, bouillon powder, cayenne pepper, and thyme.
    Set the instant pot to manual mode and set the pressure to 5 minutes. It will take a few minutes for the instant pot to come to pressure.
    When the timer goes off, quick-release by turning the steam release knob from sealing to venting.
    Switch the instant pot back to sauté mode.
    Add the heavy cream and cream cheese and stir.
    Let the soup simmer and thicken some more.
    Blend using an immersion blender or regular blender.
    Put back in the instant pot and adjust seasoning, if you wish.
    Simmer for a few more minutes and serve.

name: Akara (Bean Cakes)
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Peel the Beans.
Soak beans in water for a few minutes.
Rub between palms to remove the skin.
Rinse repeatedly until skins are removed.
Drain clean peeled beans.
Step 2: Blend the Mixture -  Blend beans with:
-Pepper
-Onion
-Small amount of water
Blend till smooth but thick (not watery).
Step 3:  Aerate the Batter (Very Important) - 
Pour mixture into a bowl.
Use a spoon or whisk to beat it vigorously for a few minutes.
This adds air → makes akara fluffy inside.
Step 4: Season
-Add salt.
-Mix well.
-Taste a small drop to adjust.
Step 5: Frying
-Heat oil in a deep pan (medium heat)
-Scoop batter with spoon or hand
-Drop gently into oil
Fry till:
-Golden brown outside
-Flip to cook evenly
Step 6: Drain
-Remove and place on paper towel.
-Let excess oil drain.

name: Seafood Okra Soup
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Prepare Ingredients
Wash and cut fish into pieces
Clean shrimp and remove veins
Wash and chop okra (or blend lightly for more draw)
Slice onions and blend pepper
Step 2: Cook the Base
Pour stock into a pot
Add onions, pepper, seasoning cubes, salt
Bring to a boil
Step 3: Add Fish
Add fish first (it takes longer to cook)
Cook for about 5–7 minutes
Be gentle to avoid breaking the fish.
Step 4: Add Other Seafood
Add shrimp, crab, periwinkle
Cook for 3–5 minutes (seafood cooks fast)
Step 5: Add Palm Oil
Pour in palm oil
Stir gently and allow it to combine
Step 6: Add Okra
Add chopped/blended okra
Stir well — you’ll notice the draw (slimy texture) forming
Step 7: Add Vegetables
Add vegetable or spinach
Stir and cook for 2–3 minutes (don’t overcook).
Step 8: Final Taste & Simmer
Taste and adjust salt/seasoning
Let it simmer briefly (2–3 minutes).

name: Native Seafood Pepper Soup
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Prepare the Seafood-
-Wash fish and cut into pieces
-Clean shrimp (remove vein)
-Wash crab/periwinkle thoroughly
Step 2: Start the Base.
-Pour water into a pot
Add:
-Onion (sliced)
-Blended pepper
-Ground pepper soup spices
-Seasoning cubes and salt
-Bring to a boil
Step 3: Add Fish Fir- prevent breaking
Step 4: Add Other Seafood-
-Add shrimp, crab, periwinkle
-Cook for another 3–5 minutes
Step 5: Add Leaves-
Add scent leaves or uziza leaves
Stir gently
Step 6: Final Simmer-
-Taste and adjust seasoning
-Let it simmer for 2–3 minutes
 Step 7: Serve
-Serve hot (very hot 🔥)
-Best enjoyed with:
-Agidi
-White rice
-Or just drink as spicy broth

name: Seafood Jollof Rice
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Prepare the Base Blend
-Blend: Tomatoes, Red bell pepper, Scotch bonnet, Onion. Set aside
Step 2: Prepare the Seafood
-Clean shrimp
-Wash and cut fish. Season lightly with salt, pepper, garlic.
-Lightly fry or grill fish & shrimp (optional but boosts flavor)
-Set aside
Step 3: Fry the Pepper Base.
-Heat oil in a pot
-Add chopped onions
-Add tomato paste and fry for 2–3 minutes
-Pour in blended mix.
Fry until it thickens and loses sour taste
(Oil should begin to separate at the top)
Step 4: Season the Sauce
-Add: Curry, Thyme, Seasoning cubes, Salt
Bay leaves.
-Stir and fry briefly.
Step 5: Add Stock
-Pour in fish/chicken stock
-Mix well and bring to a boil
Step 6: Add Rice
-Add washed parboiled rice
-Stir to coat with sauce
-Cover and cook on low heat
Step 7: Add Seafood
When rice is about 70% cooked, add:
Shrimp, Fish, Other seafood
Cover and allow to finish cooking
Step 8: Steam & Smoky Finish
-Reduce heat to low
-Allow rice to steam (no too much stirring)
-Let it slightly burn at the bottom for that party jollof smoky flavor
Step 9: Final Touch
-Add butter (optional for richness)
-Add sliced onions or spring onions
-Stir gently

name: Seafood Chowder
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Prepare Ingredients-
-Cut fish into bite-size chunks
-Clean shrimp (devein)
-Dice potatoes and carrots
-Chop onions and garlic
Step 2: Build the Flavor Base -
-Melt butter in a pot
-Add onions → sauté till soft
-Add garlic → stir till fragrant
Step 3: Add Vegetables - 
-Add potatoes and carrots
-Stir for 2–3 minutes
Step 4: Create Thickness (Roux) -
-Sprinkle flour into the pot
-Stir continuously for 1–2 minutes
-This helps thicken the chowder
Step 5: Add Liquid -
-Slowly pour in stock while stirring
-Add milk (and cream if using)
-Stir to avoid lumps
-Bring to a gentle simmer
Step 6: Add Seafood - 
-Add fish first → cook for 3–5 minutes
-Add shrimp and other seafood
-Cook for another 2–3 minutes
Step 7: Add Extras - 
-Add sweet corn (optional)
-Add thyme or parsley
-Season with salt and pepper
Step 8: Simmer -
Let it simmer till:
-Potatoes are soft
-Soup thickens to creamy consistency

name: Singapore Noodles
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions, then rinse with cold water to stop the cooking process.
Set aside.
Step 2: . Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and sliced onion, and cook until
they start to soften.
Step 3: Add the sliced bell pepper and carrot to the skillet, and stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes until they are slightly tender.
Step 4: Push the vegetables to one side of the skillet, and pour the beaten eggs into the empty side. Scramble the eggs until
cooked through, then mix them with the vegetables.
Step 5: Add the cooked shrimp, chicken, or tofu (optional) to the skillet, and stir to combine with the vegetables
and eggs
Step 6: . In a small bowl, mix together the curry powder, soy sauce, and oyster sauce (if using). Pour the sauce mixture over the
ingredients in the skillet, and stir to coat everything evenly
Step 7: Add the cooked rice noodles to the skillet, and gently toss them with the other ingredients to coat them in the sauce.
Stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes to heat up the noodles.
Step 8: Season with salt and pepper to taste, and adjust the seasoning and sauce according to your preference.
Step 9: Serve the Singapore noodles hot, garnished with fresh cilantro or green onions if desired.

name: Alfredo  pasta
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Cook the fettuccine pasta according to the package instructions in a pot of salted boiling water. Drain the cooked pasta
and set aside.
Step 2: In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for about 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Step 3: Pour the heavy cream into the skillet with the melted butter and garlic. Stir well and bring the mixture to a simmer. Let it
simmer for about 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Step 4: Reduce the heat to low and gradually whisk in the grated Parmesan cheese. Continue whisking until the cheese is fully
melted and the sauce becomes smooth and creamy. This should take about 3-4 minutes.
Step 5: Season the Alfredo sauce with salt and pepper to taste. Be careful with adding salt as the Parmesan cheese is already
salty.
Step 6: Add the cooked fettuccine pasta to the skillet with the Alfredo sauce. Toss the pasta thoroughly with the sauce until it is
well coated.
Step 7: Cook the pasta in the sauce for another 1-2 minutes to heat it through and allow the flavors to combine.
Step 8: Remove the skillet from the heat and let it sit for a few minutes to thicken the sauce slightly.
Step 9: Serve the Alfredo pasta hot, garnished with fresh parsley if desired.

name: Chili Rice Noodles
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Cook the rice noodles according to package instructions until they are al dente. Drain and set aside.
Step 2: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and minced garlic, and sauté until
they become fragrant and slightly golden, which should take about 2-3 minutes.
Step 3: Add the sliced bell pepper, julienned carrot, and sliced cabbage to the skillet. Stir-fry the vegetables for about 2-3
minutes until they are slightly softened.
Step 4: Add the thinly sliced red chili pepper to the skillet. Adjust the amount of chili according to your spice preference. Stir-fry
for another minute.
Step 5: In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, salt, and pepper. Pour the sauce mixture into the
skillet and mix well with the vegetables.
Step 6: Add the cooked rice noodles to the skillet and toss them with the sauce and vegetables until they are fully coated
Step 7: Continue stir-frying the noodles for another 2-3 minutes until they are heated through and the flavors are well combined.
Step 8: Remove the skillet from the heat and garnish the chili rice noodles with
any fresh vegetables of choice if desired.

name: Spicy Garlic Noodles
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Cook the spaghetti or noodles according to package instructions until they are al dente. Drain and set aside.
Step 2: In a large skillet or wok, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and sauté for about 1-2 minutes until
fragrant and slightly golden.
Step 3: . In a small bowl, mix together the soy sauce, sriracha sauce, brown sugar, and red pepper flakes. Whisk until the sugar is
dissolved.
Step 4: Add the sauce mixture to the skillet with the garlic. Stir well to combine.
Step 5: Add the cooked spaghetti or noodles to the skillet and toss them with the sauce until they are fully coated.
Step 6: Continue stir-frying the noodles for another 2-3 minutes until they are heated through and the flavors are well combined.
Step 7: Remove the skillet from the heat and garnish the spicy garlic noodles with chopped green onions or cilantro if desired.
Step 8: Serve the spicy garlic noodles hot as a main dish or as a side dish to accompany other Asian-inspired dishes.

name: Creamy Jollof Spaghetti
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Cook the spaghetti according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Step 2: In a large skillet or pot, heat some vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and minced garlic and
sauté until they become translucent and fragrant.
Step 3: . Add the chopped red and green bell peppers and grated carrot to the skillet. Cook them for a few minutes until they
start to soften.
Step 4: Stir in the tomato puree, curry powder, paprika, dried thyme, dried rosemary, cayenne pepper (if using), salt, and pepper.
Mix well to combine the spices and coat the vegetables.
Step 5: Reduce the heat to low and pour in the heavy cream. Stir to combine the cream with the tomato mixture. Allow the
sauce to simmer for about 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally
Step 6: Add the cooked spaghetti to the skillet with the creamy jollof sauce. Mix well to combine and ensure that all the
spaghetti is coated with the sauce.
Step 7: Allow the spaghetti to cook in the sauce for a few more minutes, stirring occasionally, until it is heated through.
Step 8: Taste and adjust the seasoning if needed. You can add more salt, pepper, or spices according to your preference.
Step 9: Once the spaghetti is heated through, remove the skillet from the heat. Serve the creamy jollof spaghetti hot, garnished
with fresh herbs if desired.

name: Mac and Cheese
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Cook the elbow macaroni according to package instructions until it is al dente. Drain and set aside.
Step 2: In a large pot, melt the butter over medium heat. Once melted, add the flour and stir continuously for about 1 minute to create a roux.
Step 3: Gradually whisk in the milk, a little at a time, to incorporate it into the roux. Continue whisking until the mixture is smooth and thickened.
Step 4: Reduce the heat to low and add the shredded cheese to the pot, reserving about 1/2 cup for later. Stir constantly until the cheese is melted and the sauce is smooth.
Step 5: Season the cheese sauce with salt, pepper, and paprika (if using). Stir well to combine.
Step 6: Add the cooked macaroni to the pot with the cheese sauce. Mix well until the macaroni is evenly coated with the sauce.
Step 7: If desired, you can pour the mac and cheese into a baking dish and sprinkle the reserved shredded cheese on top. Alternatively, you can skip this step if you prefer a creamy stovetop version.
Step 8: If using breadcrumbs, you can sprinkle them on top of the cheese layer for a crispy topping.
Step 9: Optional step: If baking, preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Place the baking dish in the oven and bake for about 20
minutes, or until the top is golden and bubbling
Step 10: Once cooked, remove the mac and cheese from the oven (if baking) and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the mac and cheese hot and enjoy its cheesy goodness!

name: Meatball
preparation_steps:
Step 1: In a large bowl, combine the ground meat, breadcrumbs, grated Parmesan cheese, milk, chopped onion, minced garlic,
egg, chopped parsley, salt, black pepper, dried oregano (if using), and red pepper flakes (if using). Mix well using your
hands or a fork.
Step 2: Once the mixture is well combined, shape it into meatballs. You can use an ice cream scoop or your hands to form evenly sized meatballs. You can make them small or large, depending on your preference.
Step 3: Heat some oil in a large skillet / deep pan over medium heat (with enough oil for deep frying). Once the oil is hot, add
the meatballs to the skillet, leaving some space between them. You may need to cook them in batches depending on the size of your skillet/deep pan.
Step 4: Cook the meatballs for about 4-5 minutes on one side, then flip them over and cook for an additional 4-5 minutes until
they are browned and cooked through. You can cut into one meatball to check if it's cooked in the center.
Step 5: Once the meatballs are cooked, remove them from the skillet and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to absorb any
excess grease.
Step 6: Serve the meatballs with your favorite sauce (You can check the sauce recipes in stews and sauce section for sweet options) and enjoy them as a main dish, over pasta, in a sandwich, or any other way you prefer.

name: Bolognese sauce
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Heat olive oil in a large saucepan or skillet over medium heat. Add the onion, garlic, carrot, and celery, and sauté for
about 5 minutes until the vegetables are softened.
Step 2: Add the ground beef (or beef-pork mixture) to the pot and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces with a wooden spoon. Drain any excess fat if necessary
Step 3: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, beef or chicken broth, and red wine (if using). Mix in the tomato paste, dried oregano, dried basil, salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes. Stir well to combine.
Step 4: Bring the sauce to a simmer and reduce the heat to low. Cover and let it gently simmer for at least 1 hour (or up to 3 hours) to allow the flavors to meld together. Stir occasionally
Step 5: If desired, stir in the grated Parmesan cheese for added richness and flavor. Taste and adjust the seasoning if needed.
Step 6: Garnish with chopped fresh basil leaves for a pop of freshness and extra taste.

name: Seafood Spaghetti
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Cook the spaghetti according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Step 2: In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and diced onion. Sauté until the onion
becomes translucent.
Step 3:Stir in the crushed tomatoes, dried basil, dried oregano, salt, and pepper. Simmer the sauce for about 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Step 4: Add the mixed seafood to the skillet and cook until they are all cooked through. Be careful not to overcook the seafood, as it can become rubbery.
Step 5: Once the seafood is cooked, add the cooked spaghetti to the skillet. Toss everything together until the spaghetti is well coated with the sauce and mixed with the seafood.
Step 6:Serve the seafood spaghetti hot, garnished with fresh chopped parsley

name: Spicy Beef Penne Pasta
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Cook the penne pasta according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Step 2: In a large skillet
/ Frying pan, season and steam the ground beef over medium-high heat until cooked through. Remove any excess grease (so 
that the following ingredients don’t stick to the pan) from the skillet.
Step 3: Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the skillet
with the ground beef and cook until the onion is translucent.
Step 4: Stir in the diced tomatoes, tomato sauce, tomato paste, dried basil, dried oregano, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper. Bring the mixture to a simmer and let it cook for about 10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Step 5: Add the cooked penne pasta to the skillet and toss everything together until the pasta is coated in the sauce.
Step 6: Serve the spicy beef penne in bowls, sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese, and garnish with fresh basil leaves.
Step 7: Enjoy your spicy beef penne while hot

name: Assorted Palm-Oil Spaghetti
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Cook the spaghetti noodles according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Step 2: In a large skillet or pan, heat the palm oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and minced garlic.
Sauté until the onions turn translucent and the garlic becomes fragrant.
Step 3: Add the assorted vegetables and protein to the pan. Stir-fry until they are cooked and tender. This usually takes around 5-
7 minutes, depending on the vegetables and protein you're using.
Step 4: In a small bowl, mix together the tomato paste, curry powder, dried thyme, and paprika. Add this mixture to the skillet and stir well to combine with the vegetables and protein.
Step 5: Season with salt and pepper to taste. Adjust the spices according to your preference.
Step 6: Add the cooked spaghetti noodles to the skillet and toss well to coat them with the sauce and vegetables. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until everything is heated through.
Step 7: Serve hot and enjoy your delicious assorted palm oil spaghetti!
Feel free to garnish with fresh herbs like parsley orscent
leaf for an extra burst of flavor

name: Chicken Curry Sauce
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until it becomes soft and translucent.
Step 2: Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet. Stir and cook for about 1 minute until the mixture becomes
fragrant.
Step 3: Add the curry powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, and turmeric powder to the skillet. Stir well to coat the onions, garlic, and ginger with the spices.
Step 4: Add the chicken pieces to the skillet and cook until they are lightly browned on all sides.
Step 5: Pour in the coconut milk and chicken broth. Stir in the corn flour mix. Reduce the heat to low and let the sauce simmer
for about 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together and the chicken to cook through.
Step 6: Taste the sauce and season with salt and pepper according to your preference.
Step 7: Once the chicken is cooked and the sauce has thickened slightly, remove from heat.
Step 8: Serve the chicken curry sauce over white rice or white
spaghetti. Garnish with any veggies of choice if desired.
Enjoy your homemade chicken curry sauce! It pairs well with rice or pasta.

name: Seafood boil
preparation_steps:
Step 1: In a large pot, bring the water or seafood broth to a boil. Add the Old Bay seasoning, garlic, onion, and a squeeze of
lemon juice from one of the halves.
Step 2: Add the halved potatoes to the pot and cook them for about 10 minutes until they are slightly tender.
Step 3: Add the corn and sausage slices to the pot. Cook for another 5 minutes.
Step 4: Add your choice of seafood to the pot. Start with items that take longer to cook, such as crab legs or crawfish. Then add
shrimp, clams, and mussels. Be sure to adjust cooking times accordingly based on the recommendations for each type of seafood.
Step 5: Cook the seafood until they are cooked through. This usually takes about 4-5 minutes for shrimp and 10-12 minutes for crab legs or crawfish. The shells of the clams and mussels should open, indicating that they are done.
Step 6: Once the seafood is cooked, remove the pot from heat and drain the liquid.
Step 7: Transfer the seafood boil to a serving platter or a large tray lined with newspaper for a more casual feel.
Step 8: Serve the seafood boil with melted butter for dipping, lemon wedges for squeezing over the seafood, and additional Old Bay seasoning for sprinkling, if desired.

name: Ofe-Nsala
preparation_steps:
Step 1: inse the assorted meat thoroughly and place it in a pot. Add enough water to cover the meat and bring it to a
boil. Cook until tender and set aside.
Step 2: In a large pot, add the chopped onions and palm oil. Cook on medium heat until the onions are translucent.
Step 3: Add the fish and cook for a few minutes until it is lightly browned on both sides.
Step 4: Add the stock or water to the pot and bring it to a boil.
Step 5: Add the yam chunks and cook until they are soft and tender
Step 6: Mash some of the yam chunks with a fork to thicken the soup.
Step 7: Add the ground crayfish and uziza seeds (if using), and simmer for a few more minutes.
Step 8: Finally, add the ugu leaves or spinach and cook until the leaves are wilted.
Step 9: Taste the soup and adjust the seasoning with salt and pepper to your liking.
Step 10: Serve hot with your choice of fufu, eba, or pounded yam.

name: Ofe-Owerri
preparation_steps:
Step 1: In a large pot, add the assorted meats, seafood, chopped onions, minced garlic, and some salt. Add enough water to
cover the ingredients and let them cook until tender. This may take 30 minutes to an hour, depending on the meats used.
Skim off any foam or impurities that may rise to the top
Step 2: While the meats and seafood are cooking, prepare the cocoyam or pounded yam paste. Peel and boil the cocoyam until
soft, then pound or mash until smooth. If using pounded yam, mix with some warm water to form a paste. Set the
prepared thickener aside.
Step 3: In a separate pan, heat the palm oil over medium heat. Add the chopped red or yellow bell peppers and sauté for a few
minutes until softened.
Step 4: Add the ground crayfish,
uziza or utazi leaves (if using), and some salt and pepper. Stir well to combine.
Step 5: Transfer the sautéed ingredients into the pot with the cooked meats and seafood. Mix everything together.
Step 6: Add the chicken or beef stock to the pot. The amount of stock you use will depend on how thick or thin you prefer your
soup. Stir well.
Step 7: Bring the pot to a simmer and gradually add
spoonfuls of the prepared cocoyam or pounded yam paste, stirring continuously. This will help thicken the soup. Adjust the
thickness to your liking.
Step 8: Add the scent leaves or basil leaves (if using) and simmer for an additional 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.

name: Edikan Ikong Soup
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Wash the assorted meat and place in a pot. Add diced onions, stock cubes, and salt to taste. Cook until tender.
Step 2: If using stockfish or dry fish, add them to the pot with the meat to cook together. This will enhance the flavor
of the soup.
Step 3: While the meat is cooking, prepare the vegetables. Wash the pumpkin leaves thoroughly to remove dirt and
slice them into thin strips. Rinse the water leaves or substitutes and cut them into smaller pieces.
Step 4: Once the meat is tender, add palm oil to the pot and allow it to heat up.
Step 5: Add the vegetable leaves (pumpkin leaves and water leaves) to the pot. Stir gently to combine with the meat
and palm oil
Step 6: Simmer the mixture for about 10-15 minutes to allow the flavors to blend.
Step 7: Add ground crayfish, pepper, and adjust the seasoning according to your preference. Stir well.
Step 8: Reduce the heat and let the soup simmer for another 5-10 minutes.
Step 9: Once the soup is well combined and the vegetables are cooked, turn off the heat. Do not over cook the
vegetables so as to maintain it's freshness and colour. You can add a generous amount of grounded crayfish at this point just before it is finally done.

name: Eka soup
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Toast the sesame seeds (beniseed) in a pan until they release a nutty aroma and start to brown slightly.
Allow the seeds to cool completely to prevent them from becoming greasy when blended.
Blend the cooled seeds into a fine powder or a thick paste, often with a little onion for flavor. Some variations include blending roasted groundnuts with the seeds.
Step 2: Season your chosen meats (chicken, beef, stockfish, cow skin) with onions, salt, and seasoning cubes.
Simmer the meat in its own juices before adding water to create a broth.
Step 3: Add palm oil to the boiling meat broth.
Stir in the blended sesame mixture (powder or paste), along with ground crayfish and pepper.
Simmer the soup for 15–20 minutes, allowing the sesame seeds to thicken the broth.
Step 4: Add bitter leaf or pumpkin leaves (ugu).
Cook for an additional 2–5 minutes until the vegetables are tender.

name: Nkwobi
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Place the cow foot pieces in a pot and add enough water to cover them. Cook over medium heat for about 1 hour or until the cow foot is tender. Drain the cooked cow foot and set it aside.
Step 2: In a separate pot or saucepan, heat the palm oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until it becomes translucent.
Step 3: Dissolve the powdered potash in about 1/2 cup of warm water. Strain the potash mixture into the pot with the palm oil and onions. Stir well to combine. The potash mixture helps to tenderize the cow foot and thicken the sauce
Step 4: Add the ground crayfish, ground Ehu seeds, chopped scotch bonnet peppers, and stock cubes to the pot. Stir everything together until well combined.
Step 5: . Add the cooked cow foot to the pot and mix it with the sauce. Allow it to simmer over low heat for about 10- 15 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together. Add salt to taste.
Step 6:While the nkwobi is simmering, rinse the chopped Utazi leaves in cold water and set them aside.
Step 7: After simmering, remove the pot from heat and garnish the
nkwobi with sliced Utazi leaves and onion rings (if desired).
Step 8: Nkwobi is traditionally served in a wooden mortar or a small bowl. Serve it with chilled drinks and enjoy!

name: Peppered Ponmo
description:
Peppered Ponmo is a popular spicy Nigerian dish made with cow skin (ponmo), typically served as a side dish
or snack. Here's a recipe to make peppered ponmo:

Ingredients:
- 500g cow skin (ponmo), washed and cut into strips or cubes
- 3-4 scotch bonnet peppers (habanero peppers), chopped
- 1 onion, chopped
- 3-4 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
- 1 teaspoon thyme
- 1 teaspoon curry powder
- 1 teaspoon bouillon powder or seasoning cube
- Salt, to taste
- Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
- Fresh parsley or coriander leaves, chopped (for garnish)
preparation_steps:
STEPS:
1. In a large pot, add the cow skin (ponmo) and enough water to cover it. Bring to a boil and cook for about 30-
40 minutes or until the ponmo is soft and tender. Drain and set aside.

2. In a separate pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until
translucent.

3. Add the minced garlic and chopped scotch bonnet peppers to the pan. Cook for a few minutes until the
peppers soften and release their flavors.

4. Add the cooked cow skin (ponmo) to the pan and toss it in the pepper sauce until well coated. Stir-fry for a
few more minutes to let the flavors meld together.

5. Season with thyme, curry powder, bouillon powder (or seasoning cube), salt, and black pepper. Adjust the
spices according to your taste preference.

6. Cook for another 5-10 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent burning, until the ponmo is fully coated and
infused with the flavors.

7. Remove from heat and garnish with freshly chopped parsley or coriander leaves.

8. Serve the peppered ponmo hot as a side dish or snack. It can be enjoyed on its own or paired with rice,
bread, or yam.

Note: You can adjust the spiciness of the dish by reducing or increasing the quantity of scotch bonnet peppers
used. Also, make sure to cook the cow skin (ponmo) thoroughly to ensure it's soft and tender.

name: FRIED RICE
description: - 3 cups cooked rice (preferably leftover or cold rice, this will make it easier for the fried rice to stand well without mashing or sticking together)- 1 cup of mixed vegetables (diced carrots, peas, corn, bell peppers, etc.) 1/2 cup diced onion - 2 cloves garlic, minced - 2 tablespoons soy sauce (optional)- 1 tablespoon oyster sauce (optional) - 2 tablespoons vegetable oil - Curry and Thyme- Salt and pepper to taste - Optional: cooked meat or shrimp (diced or shredded) Note: If using either of the soy sauce or oyster sauce, you do not need bouillon cubes or any seasoning .
preparation_steps:
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan or wok over medium-high heat
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the pan and stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes until they become fragrant and slightly translucent
Add the mixed vegetables to the pan and continue stir-frying for another 2-3 minutes until they are cooked but still slightly crisp
Add in your thyme, curry and white powder (If using bouillon cubes or any additional seasoning, add it at this point), allow it cook well on low heat before adding the rice(remember you’re stir-frying, if you don’t allow the seasoning to meld properly into the sauce, it won’t mix properly into the rice and the taste won’t be well uniformed)
If using shredded meat, chicken, or shrimp, add it to the pan at this point and mix well
Add the cooked rice to the pan, breaking up any clumps with a spatula
Stir-fry everything together for a few minutes until the rice is heated through
Drizzle the soy sauce and oyster sauce (if using) over the rice
Mix well to evenly coat the rice and vegetables
Season with salt and pepper to taste
You can adjust the amount of soy sauce or oyster sauce based on your taste preference
Continue stir-frying for another 5-7 minutes on medium heat to ensure everything is well combined and heated through
Reason is just so it will last longer and taste better
You can reduce the heat if you notice any signs of burning or over - heating
Remove from heat and serve hot as a main dish or as a side with your choice of protein
That's it! You can customize this basic recipe by adding other ingredients like pineapple, diced ham, or sesame oil for added ﬂavor
Enjoy your homemade fried rice!

name: SEAFOOD FRIED RICE
description: - 3 cups cooked rice (preferably leftover rice) - 1 cup mixed seafood (shrimp, squid, crab, etc.), cleaned and deveined - 1 cup mixed vegetables (diced carrots, peas, corn, bell peppers, etc.) - 1/2 cup diced onion - 2 cloves garlic, minced - 2 tablespoons soy sauce - 1 tablespoon oyster sauce - 2 tablespoons vegetable oil - 2 eggs, beaten - Salt and pepper to taste - Optional: sesame oil and chopped green onions for garnish  - 1 cup of rice - 1 cup coconut milk - 1/2 cup water - 1/4 teaspoon salt - Optional: toasted coconut ﬂakes (For crunchiness)
preparation_steps:
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan or wok over medium-high heat
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the pan and stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes until they become fragrant and slightly translucent
Add the mixed seafood to the pan and cook for 3-4 minutes until they are cooked through and no longer translucent
Add the mixed vegetables to the pan and continue stir-frying for another 2-3 minutes until they are cooked but still slightly crisp
Push the seafood and vegetables to one side of the pan , or a dish and pour the beaten eggs into the empty side
Scramble the eggs until cooked, then mix them with the seafood and vegetables
Add the cooked rice to the pan, breaking up any clumps with a spatula
Stir-fry everything together for a few minutes until the rice is heated through
Drizzle the soy sauce and oyster sauce over the rice
Mix well to evenly coat the rice, seafood, and vegetables
Season with salt and pepper to taste
Optional: Drizzle a little sesame oil over the rice and mix well for added ﬂavor
Continue stir-frying for another 2-3 minutes to ensure everything is well combined and heated through
Remove from heat and garnish with chopped green onions if desired
Serve hot as a main dish or as a side alongside other Asian dishes
Enjoy your delicious seafood fried rice! SWEET COCONUT RICE (As a side dish)  1
Rinse the rice a few times under cold water until the water runs clear
This helps remove excess starch
In a saucepan, combine the rinsed rice, coconut milk, water, and salt
Place the saucepan over medium heat and bring the mixture to a gentle boil
Once it boils, reduce the heat to low and cover the saucepan with a lid
Let it simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and has absorbed most of the liquid
Remove the saucepan from heat and let the rice rest, covered, for an additional 5 minutes to allow it to steam and become even more tender
Fluff the rice with a fork to separate the grains
Optional: Toast coconut ﬂakes in a dry pan over medium heat until they are lightly browned and fragrant
This adds extra texture and ﬂavor to the rice
You can serve this rice with your peppered chicken, beef or asun
You can also serve it as a side dish with your tilapia or croaker barbecue
Garnish the rice with toasted coconut ﬂakes and veggies for extra sweetness and presentation
Enjoy your delicious sweet coconut rice

name: SAUTEED (WHITE) RICE AND BEANS
description: - 1 cup white rice - 1 can of beans (such as black beans, kidney beans, or pinto beans), drained and rinsed . - 1 small onion, diced - 2 cloves of garlic, minced - 1 tablespoon vegetable oil - 1 teaspoon ground cumin - 1/2 teaspoon chili powder (optional, for added spice) - Salt and pepper, to taste - Optional toppings: chopped fresh cilantro, lime wedges, shredded cheese
preparation_steps:
Rinse the white rice under cold water until the water runs clear
This helps remove excess starch
In a medium-sized saucepan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the saucepan and sauté until they become fragrant and the onion becomes translucent
Stir in the ground cumin and chili powder (if using) and cook for an additional minute to toast the spices
Add the rinsed white rice to the saucepan and stir it in with the onion and garlic mixture until the rice is well coated
Pour in 2 cups of water and add salt and pepper to taste
Bring the mixture to a boil
Once it boils, reduce the heat to low and cover the saucepan with a lid
Let the rice simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender
While the rice is cooking, drain and rinse the beans
Once the rice is cooked, ﬂuff it with a fork and stir in the beans
Heat the rice and beans mixture for another few minutes until the beans are warmed through
Taste and adjust the seasonings if needed
Serve the white rice and beans as a side dish or as a main course
Consider adding optional toppings like chopped carrots and spring onions
The taste is superb Enjoy your delicious white rice and beans with either turkey, beef, ﬁsh or    buka stew

name: JAMBALAYA RICE
description: - 2 cups long-grain white rice - 1lb (450g) chicken or sausage, cut into bite-sized pieces- 1 lb (450g) shrimp, peeled and deveined- 1 onion, diced - 1 bell pepper, diced - 2 stalks celery, diced - 3 cloves of garlic, minced - 1 can (14 oz) diced tomatoes - 3 cups chicken or vegetable broth - 2 tablespoons vegetable oil - All purpose seasoning (Any type will do)- 1 teaspoon dried thyme - 1 teaspoon paprika - 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper (adjust according to your spice preference) - Salt and pepper, to taste - Fresh parsley, for garnish - The Nigerian twist: Fried plantain - Dodo (Slice in cubes)
preparation_steps:
In a large or medium sized pot, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat
Season the chicken or sausage pieces, and cook until browned on all sides
Remove from the pot and set aside
In the same pot, add the diced onion, bell pepper, celery, and minced garlic
Sauté until the vegetables become tender, about 5-7 minutes
Stir in the all-purpose seasoning, dried thyme, paprika, and cayenne pepper
Cook for another minute to toast the spices and release their ﬂavors
Add the diced tomatoes (along with their juices) and the chicken or vegetable broth to the pot
Bring the mixture to a boil
Stir in the rice and return the chicken or sausage to the pot
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the mixture simmer for about 20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed
Note: If using shrimp, add them in the last 5 minutes of cooking, as they cook quickly
Once the rice is cooked and the shrimp are pink and opaque, remove the pot from heat
Season with salt and pepper to taste
Mix in the dodo (fried plantain cubes) for some sweet ﬁnishing
Let the Jambalaya sit for a few minutes before ﬂuﬃng it with a fork
This allows the ﬂavors to meld together
Serve the Jambalaya Rice hot, garnished with fresh parsley for added freshness and color
Enjoy the vibrant ﬂavors and spicy goodness of this delicious Jambalaya Rice dish!

name: SPICY BEEF PENNE
description: This a delicious pasta dish made with penne pasta, spicy ground beef, and a ﬂavorful tomato sauce. - 8 ounces penne pasta (Can be substituted with whatever pasta you have available) - 1 pound ground beef - 1 small onion, diced - 3 cloves garlic, minced - 1 can (14.5 ounces) diced tomatoes - 1 can (8 ounces) tomato sauce - 1 tablespoon tomato paste - 1 teaspoon dried basil - 1 teaspoon dried oregano - 1/2 teaspoon red pepper ﬂakes (adjust to taste) - Salt and pepper, to taste - Grated Parmesan cheese, for serving- Fresh basil leaves, for garnish
preparation_steps:
Cook the penne pasta according to the package instructions
Drain and set aside
In a large skillet / Frying pan, season and steam the ground beef over medium-high heat until cooked through
Remove any excess grease (so that the following ingredients don’t stick to the pan) from the skillet
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the  skillet with the ground beef and cook until the onion is translucent
Stir in the diced tomatoes, tomato sauce, tomato paste, dried basil, dried oregano, red pepper ﬂakes, salt, and pepper
Bring the mixture to a simmer and let it cook for about 10 minutes, allowing the ﬂavors to meld together
Add the cooked penne pasta to the skillet and toss everything together until the pasta is coated in the sauce
Serve the spicy beef penne in bowls, sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese, and garnish with fresh basil leaves
Enjoy your spicy beef penne while hot! Note: You can also add some chopped bell peppers or spinach to the dish for additional ﬂavor and nutrition

name: OFE-OWERRI
description: - 500g assorted meats (beef, goat meat, cow leg, tripe, etc.) - 500g assorted seafood (shrimp, prawns, crayﬁsh, etc.) - 2 cups cocoyam or pounded yam paste (as a thickener) - 1 cup oil (preferably palm oil) - 1 onion, chopped - 3-4 cloves of garlic, minced - 2 tablespoons ground crayﬁsh - 2 tablespoons ground uziza or utazi leaves (optional)- 2 bell peppers (red or yellow), chopped - 4-6 cups chicken or beef stock - Salt and pepper to taste - 4-5 scent leaves or basil leaves (optional)
preparation_steps:
In a large pot, add the assorted meats, seafood, chopped onions, minced garlic, and some salt
Add enough water to cover the ingredients and let them cook until tender
This may take 30 minutes to an hour, depending on the meats used
Skim off any foam or impurities that may rise to the top
While the meats and seafood are cooking, prepare the cocoyam or pounded yam paste
Peel and boil the cocoyam until soft, then pound or mash until smooth
If using pounded yam, mix with some warm water to form a paste
Set the prepared thickener aside
In a separate pan, heat the palm oil over medium heat
Add the chopped red or yellow bell peppers and sauté for a few minutes until softened
Add the ground crayﬁsh, uziza or utazi leaves (if using), and some salt and pepper
Stir well to combine
Transfer the sautéed ingredients into the pot with the cooked meats and seafood
Mix everything together
Add the chicken or beef stock to the pot
The amount of stock you use will depend on how thick or thin you prefer your soup
Stir well
Bring the pot to a simmer and gradually add spoonfuls of the prepared cocoyam or pounded yam paste, stirring continuously
This will help thicken the soup
Adjust the thickness to your liking
Add the scent leaves or basil leaves (if using) and simmer for an additional 5 minutes to allow the ﬂavors to meld together
Your Ofe-Owerri is now ready to be served! Enjoy it with your choice of swallow (e
, pounded yam, fufu, or eba) or with rice

name: EFO RIRO
description: - 500g assorted meats (beef, tripe, cow leg, Ponmo, liver, roundabout, shaki,etc.) - Chopped efo Tete or efo shoko (alt.spinach or kale) 2 onions, chopped - 3-4 red bell peppers, blended - 2-3 scotch bonnet peppers, blended - 1/2 cup palm oil - 2 tablespoons ground crayﬁsh - 2 stock cubes - Salt to taste- 2 tablespoons locust beans (optional) - 2 tablespoons iru (fermented locust beans)
preparation_steps:
Wash and boil the assorted meats with chopped onions, stock cubes, and salt until tender
Set aside 2
Heat the palm oil in a large pot, add the iru and allow to simmer for about 30 seconds in the palm oil, then add your chopped onions and sauté until translucent
Add the blended red bell peppers and scotch bonnet peppers, pour in the crayﬁsh powder, mix well and cook for about 10-15 minutes until the mixture reduces and releases its oil
Add the cooked assorted meats, ground crayﬁsh, locust beans, and iru to the pot
Stir well to combine the ﬂavors
Allow the mixture to simmer for about 15 minutes to allow the ﬂavors to meld together
Add the shoko or Tete (alt
spinach or kale) stir well, cover, and let it simmer for an additional 5 minutes until the vegetables are cooked and tender
You can add some additional grounded crayﬁsh at this point and mix well into the soup until you are sure that the efo RIRO is very well mixed and combined 8
Serve hot with a side of boiled rice, pounded yam, fufu, or eba

name: OGBONO SOUP
description: - 1 cup ogbono seeds, ground - 500g meat of your choice (beef, chicken, or goat meat), cut into bite-sized pieces - 1 cup assorted meat (offals such as cow tripe, liver, and kidney), optional - 1/2 cup palm oil - 1 onion, ﬁnely chopped - 2-3 cups of vegetables (spinach, bitter leaf, or pumpkin leaves), chopped - 2-3 cups of stock or water - Salt and pepper to taste - Ground crayﬁsh (optional) - Seasoning cubes (optional)
preparation_steps:
Heat the palm oil in a pot over medium heat, then add the chopped onions
Sauté until the onions are translucent
Add the meat and assorted meat (if using) to the pot and cook until browned
Add the stock or water to the pot and bring it to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer
Gradually add the ground ogbono seeds to the pot, stirring continuously to prevent lumps from forming
If desired, add ground crayﬁsh, seasoning cubes, salt, and pepper to taste
Adjust the seasoning according to your preference
Leave the pot opened or half closed and let the soup simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the ogbono seeds and meat are cooked and tender
This will make it retain it's viscosity
Stir in the chopped vegetables and let them cook for another 5 minutes
Remove from heat and serve the ogbono soup with your choice of swallow (fufu, pounded yam, or eba)
Enjoy your homemade ogbono soup!

name: Asun Jollof Rice
description: 1. 2 cups cooked rice - 1 pound goat meat, cut into small pieces - 1 onion, ﬁnely chopped - 3 cloves of garlic, minced - 2 tablespoons vegetable oil - 2 tablespoons tomato paste - 1 teaspoon paprika- 1 teaspoon cayenne pepper (adjust to your spice preference) - 1 teaspoon curry powder - 1 teaspoon dried thyme - 1 teaspoon dried rosemary - Salt and pepper, to taste - Chopped fresh cilantro or parsley for garnish (optional)
preparation_steps:
Step 1: Season the goat meat with the available seasonings and bring to a boil (with little or no water) till it is tender and tasty
Step 2: In a medium sized or large pot, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat
Step 3: Add the chopped onions and minced garlic, and sauté until they become translucent and fragrant
Step 4: Add the goat meat to the pot and stir well
Step 5: Cook for about 5-7 minutes, until the meat is browned on all sides
Step 6: In a small bowl, mix together the tomato paste, paprika, cayenne pepper, curry powder, dried thyme, dried rosemary, salt, and pepper
Step 7: Add this spice mixture to the pot with the goat meat, and mix well to ensure the meat is coated with the spices
Step 8: Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for about 7- 10 minutes, or until the goat meat is tender
Step 9: You might need to add some water during this process if the sauce becomes too thick
Step 10: Once the goat meat is tender and the ﬂavors have melded together, add the cooked rice to the pot Mix well until the rice is evenly coated with the spicy goat meat mixture
Step 10: Cook for an additional 5 minutes on low heat to allow the ﬂavors to blend
Step 11: Remove from heat and garnish with chopped carrots, spring onion, fresh cilantro or parsley if desired
Step 12: Serve hot and enjoy Asun Rice as a ﬂavorful and spicy main course
Note: Adjust the spice levels according to your taste preference
You can also add additional vegetables like bell peppers or peas to the dish if desired
