This allows the ﬂavors to meld together
Serve the Jambalaya Rice hot, garnished with fresh parsley for added freshness and color
Enjoy the vibrant ﬂavors and spicy goodness of this delicious Jambalaya Rice dish!

name: FRIED RICE
description: - 3 cups cooked rice (preferably leftover or cold rice, this will make it easier for the fried rice to stand well without mashing or sticking together)- 1 cup of mixed vegetables (diced carrots, peas, corn, bell peppers, etc.) 1/2 cup diced onion - 2 cloves garlic, minced - 2 tablespoons soy sauce (optional)- 1 tablespoon oyster sauce (optional) - 2 tablespoons vegetable oil - Curry and Thyme- Salt and pepper to taste - Optional: cooked meat or shrimp (diced or shredded) Note: If using either of the soy sauce or oyster sauce, you do not need bouillon cubes or any seasoning .
preparation_steps:
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan or wok over medium-high heat
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the pan and stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes until they become fragrant and slightly translucent
Add the mixed vegetables to the pan and continue stir-frying for another 2-3 minutes until they are cooked but still slightly crisp
Add in your thyme, curry and white powder (If using bouillon cubes or any additional seasoning, add it at this point), allow it cook well on low heat before adding the rice(remember you’re stir-frying, if you don’t allow the seasoning to meld properly into the sauce, it won’t mix properly into the rice and the taste won’t be well uniformed)
If using shredded meat, chicken, or shrimp, add it to the pan at this point and mix well
Add the cooked rice to the pan, breaking up any clumps with a spatula
Stir-fry everything together for a few minutes until the rice is heated through
Drizzle the soy sauce and oyster sauce (if using) over the rice
Mix well to evenly coat the rice and vegetables
Season with salt and pepper to taste
You can adjust the amount of soy sauce or oyster sauce based on your taste preference
Continue stir-frying for another 5-7 minutes on medium heat to ensure everything is well combined and heated through
Reason is just so it will last longer and taste better
You can reduce the heat if you notice any signs of burning or over - heating
Remove from heat and serve hot as a main dish or as a side with your choice of protein
That's it! You can customize this basic recipe by adding other ingredients like pineapple, diced ham, or sesame oil for added ﬂavor
Enjoy your homemade fried rice!

name: SEAFOOD FRIED RICE
description: - 3 cups cooked rice (preferably leftover rice) - 1 cup mixed seafood (shrimp, squid, crab, etc.), cleaned and deveined - 1 cup mixed vegetables (diced carrots, peas, corn, bell peppers, etc.) - 1/2 cup diced onion - 2 cloves garlic, minced - 2 tablespoons soy sauce - 1 tablespoon oyster sauce - 2 tablespoons vegetable oil - 2 eggs, beaten - Salt and pepper to taste - Optional: sesame oil and chopped green onions for garnish  - 1 cup of rice - 1 cup coconut milk - 1/2 cup water - 1/4 teaspoon salt - Optional: toasted coconut ﬂakes (For crunchiness)
preparation_steps:
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan or wok over medium-high heat
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the pan and stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes until they become fragrant and slightly translucent
Add the mixed seafood to the pan and cook for 3-4 minutes until they are cooked through and no longer translucent
Add the mixed vegetables to the pan and continue stir-frying for another 2-3 minutes until they are cooked but still slightly crisp
Push the seafood and vegetables to one side of the pan , or a dish and pour the beaten eggs into the empty side
Scramble the eggs until cooked, then mix them with the seafood and vegetables
Add the cooked rice to the pan, breaking up any clumps with a spatula
Stir-fry everything together for a few minutes until the rice is heated through
Drizzle the soy sauce and oyster sauce over the rice
Mix well to evenly coat the rice, seafood, and vegetables
Season with salt and pepper to taste
Optional: Drizzle a little sesame oil over the rice and mix well for added ﬂavor
Continue stir-frying for another 2-3 minutes to ensure everything is well combined and heated through
Remove from heat and garnish with chopped green onions if desired
Serve hot as a main dish or as a side alongside other Asian dishes
Enjoy your delicious seafood fried rice! SWEET COCONUT RICE (As a side dish)  1
Rinse the rice a few times under cold water until the water runs clear
This helps remove excess starch
In a saucepan, combine the rinsed rice, coconut milk, water, and salt
Place the saucepan over medium heat and bring the mixture to a gentle boil
Once it boils, reduce the heat to low and cover the saucepan with a lid
Let it simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and has absorbed most of the liquid
Remove the saucepan from heat and let the rice rest, covered, for an additional 5 minutes to allow it to steam and become even more tender
Fluff the rice with a fork to separate the grains
Optional: Toast coconut ﬂakes in a dry pan over medium heat until they are lightly browned and fragrant
This adds extra texture and ﬂavor to the rice
You can serve this rice with your peppered chicken, beef or asun
You can also serve it as a side dish with your tilapia or croaker barbecue
Garnish the rice with toasted coconut ﬂakes and veggies for extra sweetness and presentation
Enjoy your delicious sweet coconut rice

name: SAUTEED (WHITE) RICE AND BEANS
description: - 1 cup white rice - 1 can of beans (such as black beans, kidney beans, or pinto beans), drained and rinsed . - 1 small onion, diced - 2 cloves of garlic, minced - 1 tablespoon vegetable oil - 1 teaspoon ground cumin - 1/2 teaspoon chili powder (optional, for added spice) - Salt and pepper, to taste - Optional toppings: chopped fresh cilantro, lime wedges, shredded cheese
preparation_steps:
Rinse the white rice under cold water until the water runs clear
This helps remove excess starch
In a medium-sized saucepan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the saucepan and sauté until they become fragrant and the onion becomes translucent
Stir in the ground cumin and chili powder (if using) and cook for an additional minute to toast the spices
Add the rinsed white rice to the saucepan and stir it in with the onion and garlic mixture until the rice is well coated
Pour in 2 cups of water and add salt and pepper to taste
Bring the mixture to a boil
Once it boils, reduce the heat to low and cover the saucepan with a lid
Let the rice simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender
While the rice is cooking, drain and rinse the beans
Once the rice is cooked, ﬂuff it with a fork and stir in the beans
Heat the rice and beans mixture for another few minutes until the beans are warmed through
Taste and adjust the seasonings if needed
Serve the white rice and beans as a side dish or as a main course
Consider adding optional toppings like chopped carrots and spring onions
The taste is superb Enjoy your delicious white rice and beans with either turkey, beef, ﬁsh or    buka stew

name: JAMBALAYA RICE
description: - 2 cups long-grain white rice - 1lb (450g) chicken or sausage, cut into bite-sized pieces- 1 lb (450g) shrimp, peeled and deveined- 1 onion, diced - 1 bell pepper, diced - 2 stalks celery, diced - 3 cloves of garlic, minced - 1 can (14 oz) diced tomatoes - 3 cups chicken or vegetable broth - 2 tablespoons vegetable oil - All purpose seasoning (Any type will do)- 1 teaspoon dried thyme - 1 teaspoon paprika - 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper (adjust according to your spice preference) - Salt and pepper, to taste - Fresh parsley, for garnish - The Nigerian twist: Fried plantain - Dodo (Slice in cubes)
preparation_steps:
In a large or medium sized pot, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat
Season the chicken or sausage pieces, and cook until browned on all sides
Remove from the pot and set aside
In the same pot, add the diced onion, bell pepper, celery, and minced garlic
Sauté until the vegetables become tender, about 5-7 minutes
Stir in the all-purpose seasoning, dried thyme, paprika, and cayenne pepper
Cook for another minute to toast the spices and release their ﬂavors
Add the diced tomatoes (along with their juices) and the chicken or vegetable broth to the pot
Bring the mixture to a boil
Stir in the rice and return the chicken or sausage to the pot
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the mixture simmer for about 20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed
Note: If using shrimp, add them in the last 5 minutes of cooking, as they cook quickly
Once the rice is cooked and the shrimp are pink and opaque, remove the pot from heat
Season with salt and pepper to taste
Mix in the dodo (fried plantain cubes) for some sweet ﬁnishing
Let the Jambalaya sit for a few minutes before ﬂuﬃng it with a fork
This allows the ﬂavors to meld together
Serve the Jambalaya Rice hot, garnished with fresh parsley for added freshness and color
Enjoy the vibrant ﬂavors and spicy goodness of this delicious Jambalaya Rice dish!

name: SPICY BEEF PENNE
description: This a delicious pasta dish made with penne pasta, spicy ground beef, and a ﬂavorful tomato sauce. - 8 ounces penne pasta (Can be substituted with whatever pasta you have available) - 1 pound ground beef - 1 small onion, diced - 3 cloves garlic, minced - 1 can (14.5 ounces) diced tomatoes - 1 can (8 ounces) tomato sauce - 1 tablespoon tomato paste - 1 teaspoon dried basil - 1 teaspoon dried oregano - 1/2 teaspoon red pepper ﬂakes (adjust to taste) - Salt and pepper, to taste - Grated Parmesan cheese, for serving- Fresh basil leaves, for garnish
preparation_steps:
Cook the penne pasta according to the package instructions
Drain and set aside
In a large skillet / Frying pan, season and steam the ground beef over medium-high heat until cooked through
Remove any excess grease (so that the following ingredients don’t stick to the pan) from the skillet
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the  skillet with the ground beef and cook until the onion is translucent
Stir in the diced tomatoes, tomato sauce, tomato paste, dried basil, dried oregano, red pepper ﬂakes, salt, and pepper
Bring the mixture to a simmer and let it cook for about 10 minutes, allowing the ﬂavors to meld together
Add the cooked penne pasta to the skillet and toss everything together until the pasta is coated in the sauce
Serve the spicy beef penne in bowls, sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese, and garnish with fresh basil leaves
Enjoy your spicy beef penne while hot! Note: You can also add some chopped bell peppers or spinach to the dish for additional ﬂavor and nutrition

name: OFE-OWERRI
description: - 500g assorted meats (beef, goat meat, cow leg, tripe, etc.) - 500g assorted seafood (shrimp, prawns, crayﬁsh, etc.) - 2 cups cocoyam or pounded yam paste (as a thickener) - 1 cup oil (preferably palm oil) - 1 onion, chopped - 3-4 cloves of garlic, minced - 2 tablespoons ground crayﬁsh - 2 tablespoons ground uziza or utazi leaves (optional)- 2 bell peppers (red or yellow), chopped - 4-6 cups chicken or beef stock - Salt and pepper to taste - 4-5 scent leaves or basil leaves (optional)
preparation_steps:
In a large pot, add the assorted meats, seafood, chopped onions, minced garlic, and some salt
Add enough water to cover the ingredients and let them cook until tender
This may take 30 minutes to an hour, depending on the meats used
Skim off any foam or impurities that may rise to the top
While the meats and seafood are cooking, prepare the cocoyam or pounded yam paste
Peel and boil the cocoyam until soft, then pound or mash until smooth
If using pounded yam, mix with some warm water to form a paste
Set the prepared thickener aside
In a separate pan, heat the palm oil over medium heat
Add the chopped red or yellow bell peppers and sauté for a few minutes until softened
Add the ground crayﬁsh, uziza or utazi leaves (if using), and some salt and pepper
Stir well to combine
Transfer the sautéed ingredients into the pot with the cooked meats and seafood
Mix everything together
Add the chicken or beef stock to the pot
The amount of stock you use will depend on how thick or thin you prefer your soup
Stir well
Bring the pot to a simmer and gradually add spoonfuls of the prepared cocoyam or pounded yam paste, stirring continuously
This will help thicken the soup
Adjust the thickness to your liking
Add the scent leaves or basil leaves (if using) and simmer for an additional 5 minutes to allow the ﬂavors to meld together
Your Ofe-Owerri is now ready to be served! Enjoy it with your choice of swallow (e
, pounded yam, fufu, or eba) or with rice

name: EFO RIRO
description: - 500g assorted meats (beef, tripe, cow leg, Ponmo, liver, roundabout, shaki,etc.) - Chopped efo Tete or efo shoko (alt.spinach or kale) 2 onions, chopped - 3-4 red bell peppers, blended - 2-3 scotch bonnet peppers, blended - 1/2 cup palm oil - 2 tablespoons ground crayﬁsh - 2 stock cubes - Salt to taste- 2 tablespoons locust beans (optional) - 2 tablespoons iru (fermented locust beans)
preparation_steps:
Wash and boil the assorted meats with chopped onions, stock cubes, and salt until tender
Set aside 2
Heat the palm oil in a large pot, add the iru and allow to simmer for about 30 seconds in the palm oil, then add your chopped onions and sauté until translucent
Add the blended red bell peppers and scotch bonnet peppers, pour in the crayﬁsh powder, mix well and cook for about 10-15 minutes until the mixture reduces and releases its oil
Add the cooked assorted meats, ground crayﬁsh, locust beans, and iru to the pot
Stir well to combine the ﬂavors
Allow the mixture to simmer for about 15 minutes to allow the ﬂavors to meld together
Add the shoko or Tete (alt
spinach or kale) stir well, cover, and let it simmer for an additional 5 minutes until the vegetables are cooked and tender
You can add some additional grounded crayﬁsh at this point and mix well into the soup until you are sure that the efo RIRO is very well mixed and combined 8
Serve hot with a side of boiled rice, pounded yam, fufu, or eba

name: OGBONO SOUP
description: - 1 cup ogbono seeds, ground - 500g meat of your choice (beef, chicken, or goat meat), cut into bite-sized pieces - 1 cup assorted meat (offals such as cow tripe, liver, and kidney), optional - 1/2 cup palm oil - 1 onion, ﬁnely chopped - 2-3 cups of vegetables (spinach, bitter leaf, or pumpkin leaves), chopped - 2-3 cups of stock or water - Salt and pepper to taste - Ground crayﬁsh (optional) - Seasoning cubes (optional)
preparation_steps:
Heat the palm oil in a pot over medium heat, then add the chopped onions
Sauté until the onions are translucent
Add the meat and assorted meat (if using) to the pot and cook until browned
Add the stock or water to the pot and bring it to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer
Gradually add the ground ogbono seeds to the pot, stirring continuously to prevent lumps from forming
If desired, add ground crayﬁsh, seasoning cubes, salt, and pepper to taste
Adjust the seasoning according to your preference
Leave the pot opened or half closed and let the soup simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the ogbono seeds and meat are cooked and tender
This will make it retain it's viscosity
Stir in the chopped vegetables and let them cook for another 5 minutes
Remove from heat and serve the ogbono soup with your choice of swallow (fufu, pounded yam, or eba)
Enjoy your homemade ogbono soup!

name: OFE-NSALA
description: - 500g fresh ﬁsh (tilapia or catﬁsh) - 1 medium-sized yam, peeled and cut into small chunks - 2 tablespoons palm oil - 1 onion, ﬁnely chopped - 2 teaspoons ground crayﬁsh - 2 teaspoons ground uziza seeds (optional) - 2 cups ugu (ﬂuted pumpkin) leaves or spinach, chopped - 2 cups of stock or water - Salt and pepper to taste  - 500g assorted meat (such as cow tripe, beef, goat meat, cow skin), cleaned and cut into bite-sized pieces - 2 tablespoons ground crayﬁsh - 2 tablespoons uziza seeds (optional) - 1 onion, ﬁnely chopped - 2 tablespoons palm oil - 2 cups of stock or water - Salt and pepper to taste - 1 teaspoon ogiri or ogiri okpei (traditional Igbo seasoning, optional) - 2 cups ugu (ﬂuted pumpkin) leaves or spinach, chopped
preparation_steps:
Clean and season the ﬁsh with salt and pepper, then set it aside
In a large pot, add the chopped onions and palm oil
Cook on medium heat until the onions are translucent
Add the ﬁsh and cook for a few minutes until it is lightly browned on both sides
Add the stock or water to the pot and bring it to a boil
Add the yam chunks and cook until they are soft and tender
Mash some of the yam chunks with a fork to thicken the soup
Add the ground crayﬁsh and uziza seeds (if using), and simmer for a few more minutes
Finally, add the ugu leaves or spinach and cook until the leaves are wilted
Taste the soup and adjust the seasoning with salt and pepper to your liking
Serve hot with your choice of fufu, eba, or pounded yam
To make Ofe-nsala soup with assorted meat, you can follow these steps:  1
Rinse the assorted meat thoroughly and place it in a pot
Add enough water to cover the meat and bring it to a boil
Cook until tender and set aside
In a mortar or blender, grind the crayﬁsh and uziza seeds (if using) to a ﬁne powder and set aside
In a large pot, heat the palm oil over medium heat
Add the chopped onions and sauté until they are translucent
Add the cooked assorted meat to the pot and stir well to combine with the onions
Pour in the stock or water, ensuring it covers the meat
Bring it to a simmer
Add the ground crayﬁsh and uziza seeds (if using) to the pot
Stir well to incorporate the ﬂavors
Season the soup with salt, pepper, and ogiri (if using)
Adjust the seasoning according to your taste
Allow the soup to simmer over low heat for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the ﬂavors to meld together
Optional: In a small frying pan, heat the palm oil and add ogiri or ugba (ukpaka)
Stir-fry for a few minutes until fragrant, then add it to the boiling soup and mix well
Adjust the seasoning if necessary and let it simmer for another 5 minutes
Finally, add the chopped ugu leaves or spinach to the pot
Stir well and let it cook for another 5 minutes until the leaves wilt and become tender
Taste and adjust the seasoning if needed
Serve the Ofe-nsala soup hot with your choice of fufu (pounded yam or cassava), rice, or eba
Note: Ofe-nsala can be customized by adding other ingredients such as mushrooms, scent leaves, or periwinkle
Feel free to experiment and adjust the recipe to your taste
Additionally, the shredded utazi leaves can be replaced with bitter leaf if utazi is not available

name: OFE -AKWU
description: - 2 cups of palm fruits or 1 can of palm fruit concentrate - Assorted meats (goat meat, beef, chicken, or ﬁsh) - 1 onion, chopped - 3 cloves of garlic, minced - 2 tablespoons ground crayﬁsh - 2 stock cubes - Salt to taste - 2 cups of water - 2 tablespoons of ground fresh pepper or ground spicy powder (optional) - Ogiri or ogiri okpei (optional, for ﬂavor)- Uziza or scent leaves, chopped (optional, for garnish)
preparation_steps:
If using fresh palm fruits, extract the oil by boiling them in water until tender
Then, pound or blend the fruits and strain to obtain the palm fruit concentrate
If using canned palm fruit concentrate, skip this step
Wash and season the assorted meats with onions, garlic, stock cubes, ground crayﬁsh, and salt
Boil until tender
In a separate pot, add the palm fruit concentrate or canned palm fruit concentrate with water
Bring the palm fruit mixture to a boil and let it simmer for about 20 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent burning
Add the cooked meats to the palm fruit mixture, along with any remaining stock from the meat
If desired, add ground fresh pepper or spicy powder for heat and ogiri or ogiri okpei for ﬂavor
Stir well
Allow the soup to simmer for another 15-20 minutes, until the ﬂavors are well combined
Taste and adjust the seasoning if necessary
If using uziza or scent leaves, add them to the soup and let it simmer for a few more minutes
Remove from heat and serve hot with your choice of swallow (e
, pounded yam, fufu, or eba) or rice
Note: Ofe-akwu can be personalized by adding other ingredients such as crayﬁsh, stockﬁsh, or periwinkle
Feel free to experiment and adjust the recipe to your taste
Additionally, if you prefer a thicker consistency, you can simmer the soup for a bit longer until it reaches your desired thickness

name: SEAFOOD OKRO
description: - 2 cups of sliced okra - Assorted seafood (shrimp, crab, ﬁsh, or any combination you prefer) - 1 onion (chopped) - 2-3 scotch bonnet peppers (chopped) - 3-4 tablespoons of palm oil - A generous amount of grounded crayﬁsh - Stock cubes (such as Maggi or Knorr) - Salt and pepper (to taste) - Water or ﬁsh stock
preparation_steps:
Heat the palm oil in a pot over medium heat
Add the chopped onion and sauté until it becomes translucent
Add the chopped scotch bonnet peppers and sauté for another minute
Add the sliced okra to the pot and stir well to combine with the onion and peppers
Allow it to cook for a few minutes until the okra starts to release its juices
Pour in enough water or ﬁsh stock to cover the okra
You can adjust the amount of liquid depending on how thick or thin you want your soup to be
Add the assorted seafood to the pot
You can use shrimp, crab, ﬁsh, or a combination of seafood that you prefer
Ensure they are cleaned and properly seasoned beforehand
Stir in the ground crayﬁsh, stock cubes, salt, and pepper to taste
Be cautious with the salt as some seafood may already be salty
Cover the pot and let it simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the seafood is cooked through and the okra is tender
Taste the soup and adjust the seasoning if necessary
Remove from heat and serve the seafood okra soup hot with your choice of accompaniment, such as pounded yam, fufu, or eba
Enjoy your delicious seagood okro

name: OFADA SAUCE
description: - 500g assorted meats (such as beef, offal, and smoked ﬁsh) - 1 cup palm oil - 2 medium onions, chopped - 3-4 red bell peppers, blended - 5 scotch bonnet peppers (depending on your spice preference) - 4 Hake ﬁsh(Panla) -Shrimps (optional) -4 boiled eggs - 3 garlic cloves, minced - 1 tablespoon dried crayﬁsh (optional) - 1 tablespoon iru (a type of fermented locust bean)- 2 seasoning cubes - Salt to taste - Vegetable leaves (such as Ugu or spinach), chopped (optional)
preparation_steps:
Wash the assorted meats thoroughly and place them in a pot
Add some chopped onions, seasoning cubes, and salt
Cook until tender
Drain the stock but reserve it for later
Heat the palm oil in a separate pot
Add the remaining chopped onions and the iru and sauté until they become translucent
Add the blended red bell peppers, scotch bonnet peppers, and minced garlic to the pot
Cook on medium heat for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally
Add the dried crayﬁsh if desired, followed by the remaining iru (fermented locust bean)
Stir well and cook for another 5 minutes
Add the reserved meat stock to the sauce and bring to a boil
Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for about 5 minutes
Add the cooked assorted meats and boiled eggs to the sauce and simmer for an additional 10 minutes, allowing the ﬂavors to meld together
Adjust the seasoning and salt according to your taste preferences
8 Your Ofada sauce is now ready to be served! Enjoy it with Ofada rice or any other rice variety of your choice
Note: Ofada sauce is traditionally quite spicy, but you can reduce the amount of scotch bonnet peppers if you prefer a milder version
You can also had s

name: CHILLI SAUCE
description: - 10-12 medium-sized red chili peppers (you can adjust the quantity based on your spice preference) - 4 garlic cloves - 2 tablespoons white vinegar - 1 tablespoon sugar - 1 teaspoon salt- 1 cup water  - 1 cup roasted peanuts - 2 tablespoons ground ginger - 2 tablespoons ground paprika - 1 tablespoon ground cayenne pepper (adjust based on your spice preference) - 1 tablespoon garlic powder - 1 tablespoon onion powder - 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon - 1 teaspoon ground cloves - 1 teaspoon salt - 1 teaspoon bouillon powder (optional)
preparation_steps:
Begin by washing and removing the stems from the chili peppers
If you prefer a milder sauce, you can deseed the peppers as well
Place the chili peppers, garlic cloves, white vinegar, sugar, salt, and water in a blender or food processor
Blend until you achieve a smooth consistency
If you like a chunkier texture, you can pulse blend for a shorter time
Transfer the mixture to a saucepan and bring it to a boil over medium heat
Once it starts boiling, reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for about 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally
Taste the sauce and adjust the seasoning according to your preference
You can add more salt, sugar, or vinegar if desired
Remove the sauce from heat and let it cool completely
Once cooled, transfer the chili sauce into sterilized glass jars or bottles
Store in the refrigerator for up to 1 month
This can be used for as an addition to your shawarma marinade, white rice, roasted yam or barbecue sauce Note: Remember to handle chili peppers carefully as they can cause skin irritation
You might want to wear gloves while handling and deseeding them
Suya spice This is a popular Nigerian seasoning used for grilling or barbecuing meats
Here's a recipe for homemade suya spice:  1
Begin by roasting the peanuts in a dry pan over medium heat until they are fragrant and slightly browned
Let them cool completely
Once cooled, place the roasted peanuts in a blender or food processor and blend until you achieve a ﬁne powder consistency
In a mixing bowl, combine the ground peanut powder with the ground ginger, paprika, cayenne pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, cinnamon, cloves, salt, and bouillon powder (if using)
Mix well until all the spices are evenly incorporated
Taste the mixture and adjust the seasoning according to your preference
You can add more cayenne pepper for extra heat, or increase the amount of ginger or paprika if desired
Once the suya spice is well mixed, store it in an airtight container or jar
Use the suya spice to season meat before grilling or barbecuing
It is especially delicious on beef, chicken, or goat
Note: If you prefer a nut-free version, you can substitute roasted soybeans or chickpeas for the peanuts in this recipe

name: PEPPER-SOUP SPICE
description: - 2 tablespoons ground ginger - 2 tablespoons ground garlic powder - 1 tablespoon ground cayenne pepper (adjust based on your spice preference) - 2 teaspoons ground dried Cameroon pepper (you can substitute with crushed red pepper ﬂakes) - 2 teaspoons ground white pepper - 2 teaspoons ground black pepper - 2 teaspoons ground cloves - 1 teaspoon ground dried crayﬁsh (optional) - 1 teaspoon ground allspice - 1 teaspoon ground nutmeg - 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon - 1 teaspoon dried thyme - 1 teaspoon dried basil - 1 teaspoon dried curry leaves or dried bay leaves - 1 teaspoon salt
preparation_steps:
In a mixing bowl, combine all the ground spices together: ginger, garlic powder, cayenne pepper, dried Cameroon pepper (or crushed red pepper ﬂakes), white pepper, black pepper, cloves, dried crayﬁsh (if using), allspice, nutmeg, cinnamon, thyme, basil, curry leaves or bay leaves, and salt
Mix well until all the spices are evenly incorporated
Taste the mixture and adjust the seasoning according to your preference
You can add more cayenne pepper for additional heat, or increase the amount of black pepper or cloves if desired
Once the pepper-soup spice mixture is well mixed, store it in an airtight container or jar
To use, simply add the pepper-soup spice to your desired recipe for a ﬂavorful and spicy kick
Goes perfectly well with chicken, turkey, beef, catﬁsh or snail pepper soup
Note: This recipe yields a medium-spice level
Adjust the amount of cayenne pepper and other spices to your personal taste

name: ONION POWDER
description: - 4-5 medium-sized onions
preparation_steps:
Start by peeling the onions and removing the outer skin
Slice the onions into thin, even slices
Preheat your oven to its lowest temperature setting, usually around 170-180 degrees Fahrenheit (75-85 degrees Celsius)
Spread the onion slices in a single layer on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a silicone baking mat
Ensure they are evenly spread out to allow for proper drying
Place the baking sheet with the onion slices in the preheated oven
Allow the onions to dry in the oven until they become brittle and easily crumble when touched
Check on the onions occasionally to ensure they don't burn
If needed, rotate the baking sheet or adjust the oven temperature accordingly
Once the onions are fully dried and crispy, remove them from the oven and let them cool completely
Transfer the dried onion slices to a blender or a spice grinder
Grind the dried onion slices until you achieve a ﬁne powder consistency
It may take a few moments of blending or grinding to achieve the desired texture
Once ground into a powder, sift the mixture through a ﬁne-mesh sieve to remove any larger particles or lumps
Store the homemade onion powder in an airtight container, preferably a glass jar or spice container, in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight
Use the onion powder in your recipes as a seasoning, adding depth of ﬂavor to soups, stews, marinades, dressings, or any dish that could beneﬁt from the taste of onions
Alternative: You can use a non stick pan instead of an oven, just make sure it's on low heat and check regularly to avoid the onions from getting burnt
Note: It's important to ensure that the onions are fully dried before grinding them into powder
Any remaining moisture can cause the powder to clump or spoil

name: CURRY POWDER
description: - 2 tablespoons coriander seeds - 1 tablespoon cumin seeds - 1 tablespoon ground turmeric - 1 tablespoon ground fenugreek - 1 tablespoon ground ginger - 1 tablespoon ground paprika - 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon - 1 teaspoon ground cloves - 1 teaspoon ground cardamom - 1 teaspoon black peppercorns - 1 teaspoon mustard seeds (optional) - 1 teaspoon fennel seeds (optional) - 1 dried red chili pepper (optional, for heat)
preparation_steps:
In a dry frying pan over low heat, toast the coriander seeds, cumin seeds, black peppercorns, mustard seeds (if using), and fennel seeds (if using) until fragrant
This should take about 1-2 minutes
Stir occasionally to prevent burning
Transfer the toasted seeds to a spice grinder or mortar and pestle
Grind the toasted seeds until they form a ﬁne powder
In a bowl, combine the ground seed mixture with the rest of the spices: ground turmeric, ground fenugreek, ground ginger, ground paprika, ground cinnamon, ground cloves, ground cardamom, and the optional dried red chili pepper (adjust according to your desired heat level)
Mix all the spices thoroughly until they are well combined
To enhance the ﬂavors, you can further toast the spice blend in the frying pan for another 30 seconds to 1 minute
Be careful not to burn the spices
Let the homemade curry powder cool completely before transferring it to an airtight container for storage
Store the curry powder in a cool, dark place, away from direct sunlight, to maintain its ﬂavor for several months
Note: This basic recipe can be adjusted according to your taste preferences
Feel free to add or remove spices depending on your desired ﬂavor proﬁle
Additionally, you can experiment with toasting and grinding whole spices for a more intense and aromatic curry powder

name: PEERFECT NATURAL CHICKEN RUB/SEASONING
description: - 1 tablespoon dried thyme - 1 tablespoon dried rosemary - 1 tablespoon dried sage - 1 tablespoon garlic powder - 1 tablespoon onion powder - 1 teaspoon paprika - 1 teaspoon salt - 1/2 teaspoon black pepper
preparation_steps:
In a small bowl, combine all the herbs and spices: dried thyme, dried rosemary, dried sage, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper
Mix all the ingredients together until they are well blended
Taste a pinch of the seasoning mixture and adjust the levels to suit your taste preferences
You can add more salt, pepper, or any other seasoning based on your liking
Transfer the chicken seasoning to an airtight container for storage
Store it in a cool, dark place to maintain its freshness and ﬂavor
Usage: - To season chicken, rub or sprinkle the chicken seasoning all over the meat before grilling, roasting, or sautéing
- You can also mix the chicken seasoning with a little oil or lemon juice to make a marinade for your chicken before cooking
- Adjust the quantity of the chicken seasoning based on the amount of chicken you are preparing and your personal taste preferences
Note: This homemade chicken seasoning can be customized by adding or removing herbs and spices to suit your preferences
Feel free to experiment with different ﬂavors like smoked paprika, dried thyme, or herbs like tarragon or oregano

name: THE PERFECT BEEF RUB
description: - 2 tablespoons paprika - 2 tablespoons garlic powder - 1 tablespoon onion powder - 1 tablespoon dried thyme - 1 tablespoon dried rosemary - 1 tablespoon dried oregano - 1 tablespoon ground black pepper - 1 tablespoon salt - 1 teaspoon cayenne pepper (adjust according to your heat preference)
preparation_steps:
In a bowl, combine all the herbs, spices, and seasonings: paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried thyme, dried rosemary, dried oregano, ground black pepper, salt, and cayenne pepper
Mix all the ingredients together until well combined
Taste a pinch of the seasoning mixture and adjust the ﬂavorings according to your preference
You can add more salt, pepper, or other spices as desired
Transfer the beef seasoning to an airtight container for storage
Store it in a cool, dark place to maintain freshness and ﬂavor
Usage: - Before cooking beef, rub or sprinkle the beef seasoning all over the meat to enhance its ﬂavor
- You can use this seasoning for different cuts of beef such as steaks, roasts, ground beef, or any other beef dishes
- Adjust the quantity of the beef seasoning based on the size of the meat and personal preferences
Note: Feel free to customize this beef seasoning recipe by adding or removing herbs and spices to suit your taste
You can experiment with additional ﬂavors like smoked paprika, chili powder, or mustard powder to give it a unique twist

name: HOME-MADE CHILLI POWDER
description: - 4-6 dried chili peppers - 2 tablespoons paprika - 1 tablespoon cumin seeds - 1 tablespoon garlic powder - 1 tablespoon onion powder - 1 teaspoon dried oregano - 1 teaspoon salt - Optional: 1 teaspoon cayenne pepper (for extra heat)
preparation_steps:
Begin by removing the stems, seeds, and membranes from the dried chili peppers
You can do this by cracking them open and shaking out the seeds, or by cutting them open and scraping out the insides
Heat a dry skillet or pan over medium heat
Add the dried chili peppers to the skillet and toast them for a few minutes until they become fragrant
Be sure to keep an eye on them and turn them occasionally to prevent burning
Once toasted, remove the chili peppers from the heat and allow them to cool down
Once cooled, break them up into smaller pieces
In a spice grinder or blender, add the toasted chili peppers along with the paprika, cumin seeds, garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano, salt, and cayenne pepper (if using)
Blend the ingredients until you achieve a ﬁne powder
If using a blender, you may need to stop and scrape down the sides a few times to ensure everything is incorporated evenly
Once blended, give the chili powder a taste and adjust the seasonings according to your preferences
You can add more salt, cayenne pepper, or other spices if desired
Transfer the homemade chili powder to an airtight container or spice jar for storage
Store it in a cool, dark place to preserve its ﬂavor and freshness
Usage: - Use the chili powder to add heat and ﬂavor to chili con carne, soups, stews, marinades, rubs, and other spicy recipes
- Add it teaspoon by teaspoon to your dishes, tasting along the way, until you achieve the desired level of spiciness
Note: Feel free to experiment with different chili pepper varieties or adjust the proportions of the spices to suit your taste preferences
Additionally, you can consider using a combination of mild and hot chili peppers to create a custom heat level

name: GINGER POWDER
description: - Fresh ginger root
preparation_steps:
Start by peeling the ginger root using a vegetable peeler or the edge of a spoon to remove the skin
This will make it easier to grind into a powder
Once peeled, slice the ginger root into thin rounds or small pieces
The size doesn't have to be precise, but it should be small enough to dry evenly
If you have a dehydrator: - Arrange the ginger slices or pieces on the dehydrator trays
Make sure to leave space between them for proper air circulation
- Set the dehydrator temperature to around 135°F (57°C) and let the ginger dry for 6-8 hours, or until it is completely dried and crispy
If you don't have a dehydrator, you can use an oven: - Preheat your oven to its lowest temperature setting, usually around 150°F (65°C)
Avoid using high temperatures as it can cook the ginger instead of drying it
- Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or aluminum foil and spread the ginger slices or pieces on it
- Place the baking sheet in the oven and prop the door open slightly to allow moisture to escape
- Let the ginger dry in the oven for about 2-3 hours, or until it becomes completely dried and crispy
Alt: If you don't have an oven, spread in a tray and dry in the hot sun for days and then continue with the following recipes
Once the ginger is dried, remove it from the dehydrator or oven and allow it to cool completely
Grind the ginger into a ﬁne powder using a spice grinder, coffee grinder, or mortar and pestle
Pulse or grind in small batches if needed
Sieve the ground ginger powder to remove any larger or coarse particles, if desired
Store the homemade ginger powder in an airtight container in a cool, dry, and dark place
Usage: - Use the ginger powder in various culinary creations such as curries, stir-fries, baked goods, teas, smoothies, and spice blends
- You can adjust the amount of ginger powder to your taste preferences and recipes
Note: Powdered ginger will have a more concentrated ﬂavor compared to fresh ginger, so it's recommended to use it in smaller quantities

name: GARLIC POWDER
description: - Fresh garlic cloves
preparation_steps:
Start by peeling the garlic cloves and slicing them thinly
This will make them easier to dry and grind into a powder
If you have a dehydrator: - Arrange the garlic slices on the dehydrator trays
Make sure to leave space between them for proper air circulation
- Set the dehydrator temperature to around 125°F (52°C) and let the garlic dry for 8-12 hours, or until it is completely dried and brittle
If you don't have a dehydrator, you can use an oven: - Preheat your oven to its lowest temperature setting, usually around 150°F (65°C)
- Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or aluminum foil and spread the garlic slices on it
- Place the baking sheet in the oven and prop the door open slightly to allow moisture to escape
- Let the garlic dry in the oven for about 2-3 hours, or until it becomes completely dried and brittle
If you don't have an oven, use the sun method as stated in the ginger powder recipe
Once the garlic is dried, remove it from the dehydrator or oven and allow it to cool completely
Grind the dried garlic into a ﬁne powder using a spice grinder, coffee grinder, or mortar and pestle
Pulse or grind in small batches if needed
Sieve the powdered garlic to remove any larger or coarse particles, if desired
Store the homemade garlic powder in an airtight container in a cool, dry, and dark place
Usage: - Use the garlic powder as a convenient and ﬂavorful substitute for fresh garlic in various dishes such as sauces, marinades, rubs, soups, and dressings
- Keep in mind that garlic powder is more concentrated than fresh garlic, so you may need to adjust the amount used in recipes accordingly
Note: Homemade garlic powder tends to have a stronger ﬂavor compared to store-bought varieties
Adjust the quantity as per your preference and the desired intensity in your dishes

name: TUMERIC POWDER
description: - Fresh turmeric roots
preparation_steps:
Start by washing the fresh turmeric roots thoroughly to remove any dirt or impurities
You can use a scrub brush if needed
Pat dry the turmeric roots with a clean towel or paper towels
If you have a dehydrator: - Slice the turmeric roots into thin, uniform pieces
This will help in drying them evenly
- Arrange the sliced turmeric pieces on the dehydrator trays
Make sure to leave space between them for proper air circulation
- Set the dehydrator temperature to around 110°F (43°C) and let the turmeric dry for 8-12 hours, or until it is completely dried and brittle
If you don't have a dehydrator, you can use an oven: - Preheat your oven to its lowest temperature setting, usually around 150°F (65°C)
- Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or aluminum foil and spread the sliced turmeric on it
- Place the baking sheet in the oven and prop the door open slightly to allow moisture to escape
- Let the turmeric dry in the oven for about 2-3 hours, or until it becomes completely dried and brittle
Once the turmeric is dried, remove it from the dehydrator or oven and allow it to cool completely
Use a spice grinder, coffee grinder, or mortar and pestle to grind the dried turmeric into a ﬁne powder
Pulse or grind in small batches if needed
Sieve the powdered turmeric to remove any larger or coarse particles, if desired
Store the homemade turmeric powder in an airtight container in a cool, dry, and dark place
PS: Id you don't have an oven or dehydrator, use the sun method as stated in the ginger powder recipe
Usage: - Use turmeric powder as a vibrant and ﬂavorful spice in various dishes such as curries, stir-fries, rice, lentils, marinades, and smoothies
- Turmeric is also known for its health beneﬁts due to its active compound curcumin
Adding it to your recipes can provide anti-inﬂammatory and antioxidant properties
Note: Turmeric powder can stain easily, so take precautions while handling it and clean any surfaces or utensils immediately if they come into contact with the turmeric powder

name: FLAVOUR ENHANCER (ALL PURPOSE SEASONING)
description: - 2 tablespoons paprika - 2 tablespoons garlic powder - 2 tablespoons onion powder - 1 tablespoon dried oregano - 1 tablespoon dried basil - 1 tablespoon dried thyme - 1 tablespoon black pepper - 1 tablespoon sea salt - Optional: add other spices like cayenne pepper, chili powder, turmeric, or cumin to customize the ﬂavor to your liking.
preparation_steps:
In a mixing bowl, combine all the ingredients and stir well to ensure they are evenly mixed together
Taste the seasoning blend and adjust the quantities of any speciﬁc spices according to your personal preference
Once you're satisﬁed with the ﬂavor, transfer the homemade seasoning to an airtight container or a spice jar with a tight lid
Store the homemade seasoning in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight
It should stay fresh for several months
You can use this homemade seasoning blend on various dishes such as chicken, beef, ﬁsh, vegetables, soups, or sauces
Sprinkle it on before cooking or use it as a rub for marinating meats
Adjust the quantity of seasoning used based on your taste preferences and the recipe instructions
Note: Feel free to experiment and customize this basic seasoning blend by adding other spices or herbs that you enjoy

name: TOMATO PASTE
description: - 4 pounds fresh tomatoes - 1/2 teaspoon salt - Optional: 1 tablespoon lemon juice (for acidity) - Olive oil for preservation(optional)
preparation_steps:
Start by washing the tomatoes thoroughly, then score a small "X" at the bottom of each tomato using a sharp knife
This will help with the peeling process
Bring a large pot of water to a boil
Carefully place the tomatoes into the boiling water and blanch them for about 1 minute
Using a slotted spoon, remove the tomatoes from the boiling water and transfer them to a large bowl ﬁlled with ice water
Allow them to cool for a few minutes
Once cooled, remove the skins of the tomatoes by peeling them off starting from the "X" mark
The skin should come off easily
Cut the tomatoes in half and remove the seeds and excess juice
You can do this by gently squeezing each tomato or using a spoon to scoop out the seeds and juice
Place the deseeded and peeled tomatoes in a blender or food processor
Blend until smooth
Pour the pureed tomatoes into a large, deep saucepan or pot
Add the salt and lemon juice (if using) and stir well
Cook the tomato mixture over medium heat, stirring frequently to prevent sticking, until it reduces and thickens into a paste-like consistency
This can take from 30 minutes to 1 hour or more depending on the juiciness of the tomatoes and desired thickness
As the tomato mixture reduces, be sure to adjust the heat as needed to avoid scorching or burning
Stir more frequently as it thickens to prevent sticking
Once the tomato paste reaches your desired consistency (thick and spreadable), remove it from the heat and let it cool completely
Transfer the cooled tomato paste to a clean, airtight jar or container
Store it in the refrigerator (not freezer!) for up to several weeks for best freshness
Alternatively, you can freeze it in smaller portions for longer storage
Homemade tomato paste adds tremendous ﬂavor to sauces, stews, soups, and many other dishes
Adjust the salt and lemon juice according to your taste preference, and feel free to experiment with adding herbs or spices like garlic, oregano, or basil to customize the ﬂavor

name: KETCHUP
description: - 2 pounds ripe tomatoes - 1 small onion, ﬁnely chopped - 1/2 cup sugar - 1/2 cup vinegar (preferably apple cider vinegar) - 1 teaspoon salt- 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon - 1/4 teaspoon ground cloves - 1/4 teaspoon ground allspice
preparation_steps:
Start by washing the tomatoes thoroughly
Then, score a small "X" at the bottom of each tomato using a sharp knife
This will help with the peeling process
Bring a large pot of water to a boil
Carefully place the tomatoes into the boiling water and blanch them for about 1 minute
Using a slotted spoon, remove the tomatoes from the boiling water and transfer them to a large bowl ﬁlled with ice water
Allow them to cool for a few minutes
Once cooled, remove the skins of the tomatoes by peeling them off starting from the "X" mark
The skin should come off easily
Cut the tomatoes in half and remove the seeds and excess juice
You can do this by gently squeezing each tomato or using a spoon to scoop out the seeds and juice
Chop the deseeded and peeled tomatoes into small pieces
In a large saucepan, combine the chopped tomatoes, ﬁnely chopped onion, sugar, vinegar, salt, cinnamon, cloves, and allspice
Stir well to combine
Bring the mixture to a boil over medium-high heat, stirring frequently to prevent sticking or burning
Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and let the mixture simmer for about 1 to 1
5 hours, or until it thickens to your desired consistency
Stir occasionally
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool slightly
Use an immersion blender or transfer the mixture to a blender or food processor to puree it until smooth
Be careful when blending hot ingredients, and allow the mixture to cool further if necessary
Once blended, pour the ketchup into a clean, airtight jar or container
Let it cool completely before storing it in the refrigerator
Homemade ketchup can be stored in the refrigerator for up to several weeks
Feel free to adjust the sweetness, acidity, or spices according to your taste preferences
You can also experiment with adding additional ﬂavors like garlic, ginger, or chili powder to customize your homemade ketchup

name: MAYONAIISE (BAMA RECIPE)
description: - 1 egg yolk- 1 cup vegetable oil (or any light-tasting oil) - 1 tablespoon white vinegar or lemon juice - 1/2 teaspoon Dijon mustard (For that vibrant yellow colour. Mustard also makes it thicker). Can be exempted if you don\'t have.- Salt and pepper to taste
preparation_steps:
In a mixing bowl, add the egg yolk and mustard
Whisk them together until well combined
Gradually pour the vegetable oil into the egg mixture, a few drops at a time, while continuously whisking
As you whisk, the mixture will start to thicken and emulsify
Continue adding the oil slowly, in a thin and steady stream, while whisking vigorously
This slow incorporation of oil helps to create a stable and creamy mayonnaise
Once all of the oil is added and the mixture has thickened, add the vinegar or lemon juice
Whisk well to combine
Taste the mayonnaise and season with salt and pepper according to your preference
Adjust the seasoning as needed
A blender can also be used for this recipe
Just add the oil slowly while blending, after combining the egg and vinegar in the blender 6
Transfer the homemade mayonnaise to a clean jar or container
Store it in the refrigerator for up to a week
Note: It's important to use fresh and high-quality ingredients when making mayonnaise
Additionally, make sure all utensils and containers are clean and dry to prevent any contamination

name: HOME-MADE SALAD CREAM
description: - 1 cup mayonnaise - 2 tablespoons white vinegar - 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard - 1 tablespoon honey or sugar - 1 teaspoon garlic powder (optional) - Salt and pepper to taste
preparation_steps:
In a mixing bowl, combine the mayonnaise, white vinegar, Dijon mustard, honey (or sugar), and garlic powder (if using)
Whisk them together until well combined
Taste the mixture and season with salt and pepper according to your preference
Adjust the seasoning as needed
If desired, you can add additional ingredients to customize the ﬂavor
Some options include minced garlic, chopped herbs (such as parsley or dill), or a splash of lemon juice
Whisk well to incorporate any added ingredients
Transfer the homemade salad cream to a clean jar or container
It can be used immediately or stored in the refrigerator for up to a week
Serve the salad cream over your favorite salads or as a dipping sauce for vegetables
Note: If you prefer a thinner consistency, you can gradually add a small amount of water or milk to the mixture, whisking well until desired consistency is achieved

name: COLESLAW RECIPE
description: - 1 small cabbage, shredded - 2 medium-sized carrots, grated - 1 small onion, ﬁnely chopped - 1/2 cup mayonnaise - 2 tablespoons white vinegar or lemon juice - 2 tablespoons granulated sugar - Salt and pepper to taste
preparation_steps:
In a large mixing bowl, combine the shredded cabbage, grated carrots, and chopped onion
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the mayonnaise, white vinegar (or lemon juice), sugar, salt, and pepper until well combined
Adjust the seasonings according to your taste preferences
Pour the mayonnaise dressing over the cabbage mixture
Use a spoon or clean hands to toss and coat the vegetables with the dressing
Ensure all ingredients are well combined
Taste and adjust the seasoning if needed
You can add more salt, pepper, or sugar according to your preference
Cover the bowl with cling wrap or transfer the coleslaw to a sealed container
Allow the ﬂavors to meld together by refrigerating for at least 1-2 hours or overnight
Before serving, give the coleslaw a good toss to redistribute the dressing
Adjust the seasoning if needed
Serve the Nigerian coleslaw as a side dish with your favorite main course or use it as a ﬁlling for burgers, sandwiches, or wraps
Enjoy your homemade Nigerian coleslaw! It's a tasty and refreshing addition to any meal

name: SHAWARMA
description: - 1 pound boneless chicken or beef (thinly sliced) - 2 tablespoons olive oil - 4 cloves garlic (minced) - 2 teaspoons ground cumin - 2 teaspoons ground paprika - 1 teaspoon ground coriander - 1 teaspoon ground turmeric - 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon - ½ teaspoon ground ginger - Salt and pepper to taste - Juice of 1 lemon - 4 tablespoons plain yogurt - 4 tablespoons sweet chilli sauce (optional) - Flatbread or pita bread - Sliced tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and lettuce (for garnish)  - 8 pieces of chicken (a mix of drumsticks, thighs, and/or breasts) - 2 cups all-purpose ﬂour - 2 tablespoons paprika - 1 tablespoon garlic powder - 1 tablespoon onion powder - 1 tablespoon dried oregano - 1 tablespoon dried basil - 1 tablespoon salt - 1 tablespoon black pepper - 1 tablespoon dried thyme - 1 teaspoon cayenne pepper (adjust to taste) - 2 cups buttermilk - Vegetable oil for frying - Crushed cornﬂakes for crispiness
preparation_steps:
In a bowl, combine the olive oil, minced garlic, ground cumin, paprika, coriander, turmeric, cinnamon, ginger, salt, pepper, lemon juice, yogurt, and tahini sauce
Mix well to form a marinade
Add the thinly sliced chicken or beef to the marinade
Make sure all the meat is well coated
Cover the bowl and let it marinate in the fridge for at least 1 hour, or overnight for best ﬂavor
Preheat the grill or a skillet over medium-high heat
If using a skillet, add a little olive oil to prevent sticking
Cook the marinated chicken or beef for about 4-5 minutes per side, or until cooked through and slightly charred
Once cooked, remove the chicken or beef from the heat and let it rest for a few minutes
Then, thinly slice the meat into strips
Warm the ﬂatbread or pita bread in the oven or on the stovetop
To assemble the shawarma, place some sliced tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and lettuce on the warmed bread
Add a generous amount of the grilled chicken or beef on top
If desired, drizzle some additional sweet chilli sauce over the meat for extra ﬂavor
Roll up the ﬂatbread or pita bread tightly to create a wrap or sandwich
Place on a grill or non stick pan and cook each sides for 2-3 minutes each
Serve the shawarma and enjoy! Note: You can also customize your shawarma by adding garlic sauce, pickles, or other toppings of your choice
KFC-style crispy chicken:  1
In a large bowl, mix together the ﬂour, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano, dried basil, salt, pepper, dried thyme, and cayenne pepper
This will be your seasoned ﬂour mixture
In another bowl, pour the buttermilk
Dip each piece of chicken into the buttermilk, allowing any excess to drip off
Roll the chicken in the seasoned ﬂour mixture until it is well coated, and ﬁnally roll it in with the already crushed cornﬂakes
Press the coating into the chicken to ensure it sticks
Set the coated chicken aside on a separate plate
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep skillet or pan over medium-high heat
The oil should be approximately 350°F (175°C) for frying
As long as the oil is hot enough for frying
Carefully add a few pieces of chicken to the hot oil, making sure not to overcrowd the pan
Fry the chicken for about 15-20 minutes, ﬂipping halfway through, until it is golden brown and cooked through
The internal temperature should reach 165°F (75°C)
Once cooked, transfer the fried chicken to a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil
Repeat the frying process with the remaining chicken pieces
Serve the homemade KFC-style chicken hot and crispy
It pairs well with coleslaw, fried potatoes, yamarita, or any of your favorite sides
Don't forget to drizzle in some sweet chilli sauce or and dip of your choice for an elite ecstacy

name: PLANTAIN CHIPS
description: - 2 large, green, unripe plantains - Vegetable oil for frying - Salt to taste - Optional seasonings: paprika, chili powder, garlic powder, or any preferred spices/herbs
preparation_steps:
Peel the plantains by making a small cut along the length of the skin, then carefully peel it off
Slice the plantains into thin, even slices using a sharp knife or a mandoline slicer if available
Aim for slices around 1/8 to 1/4 inch thick
Heat vegetable oil in a large frying pan or deep fryer
The oil should be enough to submerge the plantain slices
Once the oil is hot (around 350°F or 175°C), add a few plantain slices into the oil, making sure not to overcrowd the pan
Fry them for about 2-3 minutes per side or until they turn golden brown
Adjust the cooking time as needed, but be careful not to burn them
Use a slotted spoon or tongs to carefully remove the fried plantains from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate or a wire rack to drain excess oil
While the plantain chips are still hot, sprinkle them with salt and any desired seasonings
Toss them gently to coat them evenly
Feel free to experiment with different spices and herbs to customize the ﬂavor
Let the plantain chips cool completely before serving to allow them to become crispier
Store the plantain chips in an airtight container or ziplock bag to maintain their freshness and crunchiness
Enjoy the homemade plantain chips as a tasty snack on their own or serve them with your favorite dips or salsas
They can be enjoyed both warm and at room temperature

name: POTATO CHIPS
description: - 2 to 3 large potatoes (russet potatoes work well) - Vegetable oil for frying - Salt to taste - Optional seasonings: paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, or any preferred spices/herbs
preparation_steps:
Scrub the potatoes thoroughly to remove any dirt or residue
Leave the skin on for added texture and ﬂavor, or peel them if preferred
Using a sharp knife or a mandoline slicer, slice the potatoes into thin, even slices
Aim for slices around 1/8 to 1/4 inch thick
Soak the slices in cold water for 15 to 30 minutes to remove excess starch and crisp them up
Meanwhile, heat vegetable oil in a deep pan or deep fryer
The oil should be at around 325°F to 350°F (163°C to 177°C)
Drain the potato slices and pat them dry using a clean kitchen towel or paper towels
Carefully add a handful of potato slices into the hot oil, making sure not to overcrowd the pan
Fry them in batches for around 2 to 3 minutes, or until they turn golden brown and crispy
Stir occasionally to ensure even frying
Use a slotted spoon or tongs to remove the fried potato chips from the oil and transfer them onto a paper towel-lined plate or a wire rack to drain excess oil
While the chips are still hot, sprinkle them with salt and any desired seasonings
Toss them gently to evenly coat the chips
Adjust the seasoning according to your taste preferences
Allow the chips to cool completely, which will help them become even crispier
Store the homemade potato chips in an airtight container to maintain their freshness and crunchiness
Enjoy the homemade potato chips as a snack on their own or serve them alongside your favorite sandwiches, dips, or burgers
Experiment with different seasonings to create a variety of ﬂavors
